company.name: HireRight Solutions, Inc.
company.business_description: HireRight Solutions (formerly USIS Commercial Services, Inc.) is a consumer reporting agency that provides background screening reports to employers throughout the United States containing public record information, including criminal histories of individuals, to assist employers in making employment-related decisions.

case_info.legal_authority: Section 5(a) of the Federal Trade Commission Act, 15 U.S.C. § 45(a); Section 13(b) of the Federal Trade Commission Act, 15 U.S.C. § 53(b); Section 16(a) of the Federal Trade Commission Act, 15 U.S.C. § 56(a); Section 621(a) of the Fair Credit Reporting Act, 15 U.S.C. § 1681s(a)

complaint.factual_background: HireRight Solutions, a consumer reporting agency providing background screening reports to employers, systematically violated the Fair Credit Reporting Act by failing to ensure accuracy of consumer reports, denying consumers access to their files, refusing to conduct proper reinvestigations of disputes, and failing to notify consumers when reporting public record information. These failures led to inaccurate reports being provided to employers, resulting in prospective employees being denied employment or employment-related benefits based on erroneous information.

counts.title: Failure to Ensure Maximum Possible Accuracy
counts.representation: Defendant was required to follow reasonable procedures to assure maximum possible accuracy of consumer report information
counts.fact: Defendant failed to follow reasonable procedures to assure that information reflected current public record status (such as expungements), failed to prevent multiple entries for the same criminal offense, and failed to prevent inclusion of obviously erroneous information such as records of other consumers with different names, dates of birth, or identifiers
counts.conclusion: By and through the acts and practices described in Paragraph 13, Defendant has violated section 607(b) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681e(b)

counts.title: Failure to Disclose Consumer File Information
counts.representation: Defendant was required to disclose to consumers, upon request, all information maintained in their consumer report files
counts.fact: Defendant either failed to provide consumers with information in their files or failed to do so upon request, with systems resulting in delays or non-fulfillment of requests due to inadequate staffing or technical problems
counts.conclusion: By and through the acts and practices described in Paragraph 16, Defendant has violated section 609(a)(1) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681g(a)(1)

counts.title: Failure to Conduct Proper Reinvestigations
counts.representation: Defendant was required to conduct reinvestigations of disputed items in a consumer's file upon receipt of a notice of dispute
counts.fact: Defendant did not conduct reinvestigations upon receipt of dispute notices, instead requiring consumers to first obtain a copy of their report before initiating reinvestigation, which delayed the process and made it more difficult for consumers
counts.conclusion: By and through the acts and practices described in Paragraph 17, Defendant has violated section 611(a)(1)(A) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681i(a)(1)(A)

counts.title: Failure to Provide Written Notice of Reinvestigation Results
counts.representation: Defendant was required to provide written notice to consumers of the results of reinvestigations
counts.fact: Defendant closed dispute reinvestigations without providing written notice of the results to affected consumers
counts.conclusion: By and through the acts and practices described in Paragraph 18, Defendant has violated section 611(a)(6) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681i(a)(6)

counts.title: Failure to Comply with Public Record Information Requirements
counts.representation: Defendant was required to either notify consumers at the time public record information was reported or maintain strict procedures to ensure the information was complete and up to date
counts.fact: Defendant neither notified consumers at the time information was reported (their multi-step notification process was not initiated until the day the report was transmitted and typically took more than one day to complete) nor maintained strict procedures to ensure information was complete and up to date
counts.conclusion: By and through the acts and practices described in Paragraph 20, Defendant has violated section 613(a)(1)-(2) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681k(a)(1)-(2)

definitions.Defendant: HireRight Solutions and its successors and assigns.
definitions.FCRA definitions: The definitions set forth in the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681a, et seq., shall apply.

provisions.title: Civil Penalty
provisions.summary: Judgment of $2.6 million is entered against Defendant as a civil penalty for FCRA violations, payable within 7 business days by electronic fund transfer.

requirements.description: Pay civil penalty of $2.6 million to Treasurer of the United States by electronic fund transfer
requirements.quoted_text: B. Defendant shall make this payment within seven (7) business days of the date of service of this Order to the Treasurer of the United States by electronic fund transfer in accordance with instructions provided by the Consumer Protection Branch, Civil Division, U.S. Department of Justice, Washington, D.C. 20530, for appropriate disposition.

requirements.description: In event of default continuing for 10 days, entire unpaid penalty plus interest becomes immediately due and payable
requirements.quoted_text: C. In the event of any default in payment, which default continues for ten (10) days beyond the due date of payment, the entire unpaid penalty, together with interest, as computed pursuant to 28 U.S.C. § 1961, from the date of default to the date of payment, shall immediately become due and payable.

requirements.description: Relinquish all dominion, control, and title to funds paid and make no claim to or demand return of funds
requirements.quoted_text: D. Defendant relinquishes all dominion, control, and title to the funds paid to the fullest extent permitted by law. Defendant shall make no claim to or demand return of the funds, directly or indirectly, through counsel or otherwise.

provisions.title: Prohibited Business Activities
provisions.summary: Defendant is permanently restrained and enjoined from failing to maintain reasonable procedures to assure maximum possible accuracy of consumer reports and from various other FCRA violations.

requirements.description: Must not fail to maintain reasonable procedures to assure maximum possible accuracy of information in consumer reports as required by section 607(b) of FCRA
requirements.quoted_text: A. Failing to maintain reasonable procedures to assure the maximum possible accuracy of the information concerning the individual about whom a consumer report relates, as required by section 607(b) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681e(b), including but not limited to:

requirements.description: Must not fail to follow reasonable procedures to assure information in consumer reports reflects current public record status, such as expungement of criminal record
requirements.quoted_text: 1. failing to follow reasonable procedures to assure that the information contained in consumer reports it furnishes reflects the current public record status of consumers' information, such as expungement of a criminal record;

requirements.description: Must not fail to follow reasonable procedures to prevent inclusion of multiple entries for the same criminal offense in a single report in a manner that would render the report inaccurate
requirements.quoted_text: 2. failing to follow reasonable procedures to prevent the inclusion of multiple entries for the same criminal offense in a single report in a manner that would render the report inaccurate in violation of section 607(b) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681e(b); and

requirements.description: Must not fail to follow reasonable procedures to prevent provision of obviously erroneous consumer report information to employers, such as information on a single consumer that includes records of other consumers with different names, dates of birth, or other identifiers
requirements.quoted_text: 3. failing to follow reasonable procedures to prevent the provision of obviously erroneous consumer report information to employers, such as purported information on a single consumer that includes records of other consumers with different names, dates of birth, or other identifiers that are available in the public record.

requirements.description: Must not fail to clearly and accurately disclose to consumer all information in consumer's file at time of request, as required by section 609(a)(1) of FCRA
requirements.quoted_text: B. Failing to, upon request, clearly and accurately disclose to a consumer all information in the consumer's file at the time of the request, as required by section 609(a)(1) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681g(a)(1).

requirements.description: Must not require consumer who disputes completeness or accuracy of information to obtain copy of consumer report before conducting reasonable reinvestigation within 30 days
requirements.quoted_text: C. Requiring that a consumer who disputes the completeness or accuracy of information in his or her file obtain a copy of his or her consumer report before Defendant will conduct a reasonable reinvestigation to determine whether the disputed information is inaccurate and record the current status of the disputed information, or delete the information from the file before the end of the 30-day period beginning on the date on which Defendant receives the notice of dispute from the consumer, in violation of section 611(a)(1)(A) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681i(a)(1)(A).

requirements.description: Must not fail to provide written notice to consumer of results of reinvestigation not later than 5 business days after completion, as required by section 611(a)(6) of FCRA
requirements.quoted_text: D. Failing to provide written notice to the consumer of the results of a reinvestigation not later than five business days after its completion, as required by section 611(a)(6) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681i(a)(6).

requirements.description: Must not fail to notify consumer when public record information is reported to user, or alternatively, must maintain strict procedures to ensure public record information likely to have adverse effect on consumer's ability to obtain employment is complete and up to date, as required by section 613(a)(1)-(2) of FCRA
requirements.quoted_text: E. Failing to notify the consumer, at the time such information is reported to the user of the information, of the fact that public record information is being reported by the consumer reporting agency, together with the name and address of the person to whom such information is reported; or, in the alternative, failing to maintain strict procedures to insure that whenever public record information which is likely to have an adverse effect on a consumer's ability to obtain employment is reported it is complete and up to date, as required by section 613(a)(1)-(2) of the FCRA, 15 U.S.C. § 1681k(a)(1)-(2).

provisions.title: Order Acknowledgments
provisions.summary: Defendant must submit acknowledgment of receipt of Order and deliver copies to officers, employees, and related entities, obtaining signed acknowledgments from recipients.

requirements.description: Submit to Commission acknowledgment of receipt of Order sworn under penalty of perjury
requirements.quoted_text: A. Defendant, within 7 days of entry of this Order, must submit to the Commission an acknowledgment of receipt of this Order sworn under penalty of perjury.

requirements.description: Deliver copy of Order to all officers, vice presidents, directors, and managers
requirements.quoted_text: B. For 3 years after entry of this Order, Defendant must deliver a copy of this Order to: (1) all of its officers, vice presidents, directors, and managers; (2) all of its employees, agents,

requirements.description: Deliver copy of Order to all employees, agents, and representatives who participate in conduct related to subject matter of Order
requirements.quoted_text: to: (1) all of its officers, vice presidents, directors, and managers; (2) all of its employees, agents, and representatives who participate in conduct related to the subject matter of the Order; and (3)

requirements.description: Deliver copy of Order to any business entity resulting from change in structure
requirements.quoted_text: B. For 3 years after entry of this Order, Defendant must deliver a copy of this Order to: (1) all of its officers, vice presidents, directors, and managers; (2) all of its employees, agents, and representatives who participate in conduct related to the subject matter of the Order; and (3) any business entity resulting from any change in structure as set forth in the Section titled Compliance Reporting. Delivery must occur within 7 days of entry of this Order for current personnel. To all others, delivery must occur before they assume their responsibilities.

requirements.description: Deliver Order to new personnel before they assume responsibilities
requirements.quoted_text: personnel. To all others, delivery must occur before they assume their responsibilities.

requirements.description: Obtain signed and dated acknowledgment of receipt of Order from each individual or entity to which Order was delivered
requirements.quoted_text: C. From each individual or entity to which Defendant delivered a copy of this Order, Defendant must obtain, within 30 days, a signed and dated acknowledgment of receipt of this Order.

provisions.title: Compliance Reporting
provisions.summary: Defendant must submit initial compliance report 180 days after entry of Order and ongoing compliance notices for 5 years regarding changes in contact information, structure, or bankruptcy filings.

requirements.description: Submit initial compliance report sworn under penalty of perjury, including contact information, business details, activities, compliance status, and Order Acknowledgments
requirements.quoted_text: A. One hundred eighty days after entry of this Order, Defendant must submit a compliance report, sworn under penalty of perjury. 1. Defendant must: (a) designate at least one telephone number and an email, physical, and postal address as points of contact, which representatives of the Commission and Plaintiff may use to communicate with Defendant; (b) identify all of Defendant's businesses by all of their names, telephone numbers, and physical, postal, email, and Internet addresses; (c) describe the activities of each business, including the products and services offered, and the means of advertising, marketing, and sales; (d) describe in detail whether and how Defendant is in compliance with each Section of this Order; and (e) provide a copy of each Order Acknowledgment obtained pursuant to this Order, unless previously submitted to the Commission.

requirements.description: Submit compliance notice sworn under penalty of perjury within 14 days of any change in designated point of contact or structure of Defendant
requirements.quoted_text: B. For 5 years following entry of this Order, Defendant must submit a compliance notice, sworn under penalty of perjury, within 14 days of any change in the following: 1. Defendant must report any change in: (a) any designated point of contact; or (b) the structure of Defendant or any entity that Defendant has any ownership interest in or directly or indirectly controls that may affect compliance obligations arising under this Order, including creation, merger, sale, or dissolution of the entity or any subsidiary, parent, or affiliate that engages in any acts or practices subject to this Order.

requirements.description: Submit notice of filing of any bankruptcy petition, insolvency proceeding, or similar proceeding by or against Defendant
requirements.quoted_text: C. Defendant must submit to the Commission notice of the filing of any bankruptcy petition, insolvency proceeding, or any similar proceeding by or against Defendant within 14 days of its filing.

requirements.description: Ensure any submission to Commission required to be sworn under penalty of perjury is true and accurate and complies with 28 U.S.C. § 1746
requirements.quoted_text: D. Any submission to the Commission required by this Order to be sworn under penalty of perjury must be true and accurate and comply with 28 U.S.C. § 1746, such as by concluding: "I declare under penalty of perjury under the laws of the United States of America that the foregoing is true and correct. Executed on: ____" and supplying the date, signatory's full name, title (if applicable), and signature.

requirements.description: Submit all required materials to Commission by email to DEbrief@ftc.gov or overnight courier to Associate Director for Enforcement
requirements.quoted_text: E. Unless otherwise directed by a Commission representative in writing, all submissions to the Commission pursuant to this Order must be emailed to DEbrief@ftc.gov or sent by overnight courier (not the U.S. Postal Service) to: Associate Director for Enforcement, Bureau of Consumer Protection, Federal Trade Commission, 600 Pennsylvania Avenue NW, Washington, DC 20580. The subject line must begin: FTC v. HireRight Solutions, Inc.

provisions.title: Recordkeeping Provisions
provisions.summary: Defendant must create and retain specific records for 5 years after entry of Order, including accounting records, personnel records, customer files, complaints, training materials, and compliance documents.

requirements.description: Maintain accounting records showing revenues from all goods or services sold, all costs incurred, and resulting net profit or loss
requirements.quoted_text: A. Accounting records showing the revenues from all goods or services sold, all costs incurred in generating those revenues, and the resulting net profit or loss;

requirements.description: Maintain personnel records showing for each person providing services their name, addresses, telephone numbers, job title or position, dates of service, and if applicable, reason for termination
requirements.quoted_text: B. Personnel records showing, for each person providing services, whether as an employee or otherwise, that person's: name, addresses, and telephone numbers; job title or position; dates of service; and, if applicable, the reason for termination;

requirements.description: Maintain customer files showing names, addresses, telephone numbers, dollar amounts paid, and quantity and description of goods or services purchased
requirements.quoted_text: C. Customer files showing the names, addresses, telephone numbers, dollar amounts paid, and the quantity and description of goods or services purchased, to the extent such information is obtained in the ordinary course of business;

requirements.description: Maintain records of complaints and refund requests, whether received directly or indirectly, and any response
requirements.quoted_text: D. Complaints and refund requests, whether received directly or indirectly, such as through a third party, and any response;

requirements.description: Maintain copies of all training materials and written policies and procedures that relate to activities alleged in Complaint or compliance with Order
requirements.quoted_text: E. Copies of all training materials and written policies and procedures that relate to Defendant's activities as alleged in the Complaint or Defendant's compliance with the provisions of this Order; and

requirements.description: Maintain all records and documents necessary to demonstrate full compliance with each provision of Order, including all submissions to Commission
requirements.quoted_text: F. All records and documents necessary to demonstrate full compliance with each provision of this Order, including all submissions to the Commission.

provisions.title: Compliance Monitoring
provisions.summary: Commission and Plaintiff are authorized to monitor Defendant's compliance through document requests, depositions, interviews, discovery, and other lawful means including posing as consumers.

requirements.description: Within 14 days of written request from Commission or Plaintiff representative, must submit additional compliance reports or requested information sworn under penalty of perjury, appear for depositions, and produce documents for inspection and copying
requirements.quoted_text: A. Within 14 days of receipt of a written request from a representative of the Commission or Plaintiff, Defendant must: submit additional compliance reports or other requested information, which must be sworn under penalty of perjury; appear for depositions; and produce documents, for inspection and copying. The Commission and Plaintiff are also authorized to obtain discovery, without further leave of court, using any of the procedures prescribed by Federal Rules of Civil Procedure 29, 30 (including telephonic depositions), 31, 33, 34, 36, 45, and 69, provided that, Defendant, after attempting to resolve a dispute without court action and for good cause shown, may file a motion with this Court seeking an order including one or more of the protections set forth in Rule 26(c).

requirements.description: Permit representatives of Commission and Plaintiff to interview any employee or other person affiliated with Defendant who has agreed to interview
requirements.quoted_text: communicate directly with Defendant. Defendant must permit representatives of the Commission and Plaintiff to interview any employee or other person affiliated with Defendant who has agreed to such an interview. The person interviewed may have counsel present.

requirements.description: Allow Commission and Plaintiff to use all lawful means including posing as consumers, suppliers, or other individuals or entities to Defendant without necessity of identification or prior notice
requirements.quoted_text: C. The Commission and Plaintiff may use all other lawful means, including posing, through its representatives, as consumers, suppliers, or other individuals or entities, to Defendant or any individual or entity affiliated with Defendant, without the necessity of identification or prior notice. Nothing in this Order limits the Commission's lawful use of compulsory process, pursuant to sections 9 and 20 of the FTC Act, 15 U.S.C. §§ 49, 57b-1.

provisions.title: Retention of Jurisdiction
provisions.summary: Court retains jurisdiction of this matter for purposes of construction, modification, and enforcement of the Order.

requirements.description: Court retains jurisdiction for purposes of construction, modification, and enforcement of Order
requirements.quoted_text: IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that this Court retains jurisdiction of this matter for purposes of construction, modification, and enforcement of this Order.

provisions.title: Costs and Attorneys' Fees
provisions.summary: Each party shall bear its own costs and attorneys' fees incurred in connection with this action.

requirements.description: Each party shall bear its own costs and attorneys' fees incurred in connection with this action
requirements.quoted_text: IT IS FURTHER ORDERED that each party shall bear its own costs and attorneys' fees incurred in connection with this action.

duration.termination_text: This Court retains jurisdiction of this matter for purposes of construction, modification, and enforcement of this Order.

takeaway_brief: HireRight systematically failed to ensure accuracy of background screening reports, denied consumers access to their own files, and refused to properly reinvestigate disputes.
takeaway_full: HireRight Solutions violated the Fair Credit Reporting Act through a pattern of systemic failures—including providing inaccurate background reports to employers, denying consumers access to their own files, refusing to conduct adequate reinvestigations of disputed information, and failing to notify consumers when reporting public record information—resulting in job applicants being denied employment based on erroneous data. The FTC required HireRight to comply with FCRA requirements for accuracy, file disclosure, and reinvestigation, and to pay a civil penalty.
